{{Quote|The amphistaff is not just a Vong's weapon, it is his most important ally. With an amphistaff in his arsenal, a Vong is never truly alone.|[[Wedge Antilles/Legends|Wedge Antilles]]|<ref name="The New Essential Guide to Weapons and Technology">''[[The New Essential Guide to Weapons and Technology]]''</ref>}}
'''Amphistaffs''' were genetically engineered serpentine creatures that served as the primary anti-personnel weapons of the [[Yuuzhan Vong]].

==Description==
Like all [[Yuuzhan Vong]] biotechnology, amphistaffs were fully organic beings native to the Yuuzhan Vong [[Yuuzhan'tar|homeworld]]. Young amphistaffs grew in trios from leathery polyps, reaching out and snatching whatever prey could be found and redirecting it into the polyp's gaping maw, known as the groundmouth, thus feeding all four entities simultaneously. Eventually the amphistaffs lengthened to around 2 to 3 meters, too large for their polyp to support them. They would break off, escaping their amphistaff breeding [[gla]], and slither into the wilderness leaving the polyp to die. Amphistaffs saw in [[Infrared/Legends|infrared]] light.

[[File:AmphistaffSpit-FF62.png|thumb|left|250px|Amphistaffs were capable of spitting their venom.]]
Fully grown amphistaffs were durable and cunning predators. [[Power gland]]s in the serpent's body could emit electrical impulses that reorganized the creature's semicrystalline cell structure. This field extended a millimeter from the amphistaff's body and created razor-thin edges capable of slashing through prey. Only [[Vonduun Skerr Kyrric|vonduun crabs]] rivaled the amphistaff in rigidity. After feeding voraciously, amphistaffs morphed into polyps to continue their [[Species/Legends|species]]' reproduction.<ref name="Traitor">''[[The New Jedi Order: Traitor]]''</ref>

If a [[Warrior caste|warrior]] captured an amphistaff as it left its polyp, the two were bound together and the amphistaff would loyally serve its new master in battle. Hand motions from the warrior coaxed the amphistaff to either harden or become pliable, for each had its own purposes. Amphistaffs had three distinct forms: a quarterstaff, a spear, or a whip. As a hardened spear with a poisonous head, the amphistaff could be hurled, further increasing its lethality. As a flexible whip, the amphistaff could trip or pin its target and even poison it.<ref name="SECR" /> Amphistaffs were one of the most durable objects known, as not even [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsabers]] could cut through them. When used as a heavy staff, the serpent would endure blow after blow from the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] weaponry and remain unfazed. The amphistaff's edge—measured in atoms—slashed through flesh with no resistance. In the blink of an eye, the amphistaff could soften and wind around the lightsaber's blade, its venomous jaws snapping and tearing at the unfortunate victim's face. Because of its organic nature, these creatures were capable of recovering from virtually any wounds. Nigh indestructible, an amphistaff could only be killed by repeated blunt trauma to or removal of the head.

[[File:Amphistaff-FF62.png|thumb|right|250px|A coiled amphistaff]]
Psychological damage was rampant in [[New Republic/Legends|Republic]] soldiers that entered ranged combat with amphistaff-armed Yuuzhan Vong. When directed, an amphistaff spat venom at a distance of 20 meters. This spray was aimed directly at the target's eyes and instantly blinded them. Poison seeped through the pores and entered the victim's bloodstream, interfering with circulation and choking off the oxygen supply. Excruciating pain accompanied twenty hours of suffering until the victim finally died. [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Mara Jade Skywalker]] eventually created a biotoxin drill that, if administered early enough, could disrupt the venom's lethal actions.

Certain types of specialized amphistaffs, or related creatures existed among the Yuuzhan Vong. [[Supreme Overlord]]s such as [[Shimrra Jamaane]] possessed uniquely gigantic variations of the typical amphistaff called [[Scepter of Power|Scepters of Power]]. [[Warmaster]]s such as [[Tsavong Lah]] and [[Nas Choka]] were granted "Batons of Rank" called [[tsaisi]]. Also there was the [[Scepter of Entreaty]] which were used when one wished to supplicate the Supreme Overlord. There also existed a species of amphistaff that could be implanted within the body which could be used by infiltrators as the amphistaff would avoid detection until the appropriate time at which point they could be used. Finally, the reptoid [[Chazrach]] [[Slavery/Legends|slaves]] were armed with [[coufee]]s, dagger like creatures, which were inferior, less flexible, and smaller versions of the amphistaffs, to compensate for their tiny stature and lower combat skills.

==History==
{{Quote|Beast of the vines, I claim you. I am the warrior, you are the weapon. Submit to my will.|[[Jariah Syn]] speaking in the [[Yuuzhan Vong (language)|Yuuzhan Vong]] and claiming an [[Heartstriker|amphistaff]] as his own|<ref name="Legacy 44">[[Legacy (2006) 44|''Legacy'' (2006) 44]]</ref>}}
In [[33 BBY/Legends|33 BBY]], [[Darth Maul/Legends|Darth Maul]] fought a Yuuzhan Vong warrior with an amphistaff during his time in [[Cog Hive Seven]].<ref name="Lockdown">''[[Maul: Lockdown]]''</ref>

[[File:Vong_swinvasion.jpg|thumb|left|200px|A Yuuzhan Vong warrior wielding an amphistaff.]]
During the Yuuzhan Vong War, the galaxy at large first learned about amphistaffs when the [[Praetorite Vong]] infiltrated [[ExGal-4]] on Belkadan where it was wielded by the advance agent [[Yomin Carr]]. This member of the [[Yuuzhan Vong empire]] used this weapon against [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Mara Jade Skywalker]].<ref name="VP">''[[The New Jedi Order: Vector Prime]]''</ref>

A number of hibernating amphistaffs were smuggled onto [[Hosk Station/Legends|Hosk Station]] in order to arm elements within the [[Givin/Legends|Givin]] government who were sympathetic to the Yuuzhan Vong. The aim was to create an uprising when the Yuuzhan Vong fleet arrived in the system thus securing the location.<ref name="BP">''[[The New Jedi Order: Balance Point]]''</ref>

[[Darth Caedus|Jacen Solo]] while onboard a Yuuzhan Vong [[Sh'rip Sh'pa|seedship]] managed to use his [[Vongsense]] and connection to animals to gather seventeen young amphistaffs to his side and help him against the Yuuzhan Vong crew onboard the ship. He used these feral amphistaffs to also slay several of the larval [[Dhuryam]] in order to sabotage the [[Terraforming/Legends|shaping]] of the planet [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]].<ref name="Traitor" />

At the [[Yuuzhan Vong War|war's]] close, a group of warriors crashed on [[Zonama Sekot/Legends|Zonama Sekot]], the Yuuzhan Vong's home. As the warriors exited their downed [[Yorik-et|coralskippers]], their amphistaffs uncoiled themselves and fled into the [[tampasi]]. All other amphistaffs were loaded onto the warships of the Yuuzhan Vong and sent crashing into Coruscant's [[Coruscant Prime/Legends|star]].<ref name="TUF">''[[The New Jedi Order: The Unifying Force]]''</ref>

In [[137 ABY]], [[Jariah Syn]] traveled to [[Wayland/Legends|Wayland]], where he managed to tame one of the native amphistaffs and used it as a weapon, which he named [[Heartstriker]].<ref name="Legacy 44" />

==Behind the scenes==
The origins of the amphistaff possibly date back as far as the [[Dark Horse invasion storyline|Bantam/Dark Horse invasion storyline]], where the (non-[[Humanoid/Legends|humanoid]]) invaders were to be armed with a bladed, anti-lightsaber weapon.<ref name="DHBoards">{{DHBoards|subdomain=boards|url=viewtopic.php?t=3394|thread=Nom Anor, DHC's "invasion" storyline and the NJO}}</ref>
